claim: AI coding workflows have three core phases: planning (vibe planning + requirements gathering), implementation (structured task execution), and validation (self-review + human review)—this mental model applies to any framework
actionable: Structure every AI coding project with a planning phase (free-form exploration, then PRD), implementation phase (task-driven execution), and validation phase (automated + manual)
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Vibe planning (free-form exploration with the AI assistant) should come before structured prompting; it helps both human and AI align on architecture and requirements
actionable: Start new projects with unstructured conversation; don't jump to formal specs—let exploration guide structure
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: The PRD (product requirements document) created after vibe planning should be high-level for new projects but detailed for existing codebase features, including integration points and architecture references
actionable: Create a markdown PRD after exploration; for existing projects, identify and document integration points (files to edit, patterns to follow)
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Context engineering applies four components: RAG (external docs), memory (conversation history), task management (breaking work into granular pieces), and prompt engineering (structuring the request)
actionable: When setting up AI coding, explicitly address all four: gather docs, maintain conversation context, create task lists, and craft clear prompts
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Slash commands turn prompts into reusable workflows; the PRP framework and GitHub spec kit are formalized versions of this same concept—understanding philosophy over copying frameworks gives flexibility
actionable: Build custom slash commands that reflect your planning-implement-validate workflow rather than force-fitting existing frameworks
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Sub-agents with isolated context windows should be used for planning (research) and validation (testing), but NOT implementation—implementation must stay in the primary context to avoid conflicting code changes
actionable: Use sub-agents for upfront research and for validators; keep all code-writing in the main agent's context window
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Breaking large implementation tasks into granular sub-tasks (managed via Archon or markdown) prevents hallucinations; if the AI tries to do too much at once, quality degrades
actionable: Limit implementation prompts to 1-3 concrete tasks at a time; use task management tools to track progress
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Global rules (system-level instructions in claude.md or equivalent) should define golden rules that apply across all interactions; they're set once during planning, not tweaked per task
actionable: Write global rules in your coding assistant config that describe high-level project principles; reference them consistently
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Code Rabbit (AI code review tool) can be integrated into PR pipelines for automated review; using an external validator prevents the main agent from reviewing its own work, improving quality
actionable: Set up Code Rabbit in your GitHub Actions or as a CLI tool; let it validate before human review
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)

claim: Human code review of AI-generated code is critical—you must be the project manager, not rely on the AI to validate everything; manually test and understand the output
actionable: Review and test all AI code before merging; use AI as a tool, not as a complete replacement for human judgment
source_title: The True Power of AI Coding - Build Your OWN Workflows (Full Guide)
